note: Canonical buildings-demo rule examples — the SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH for both the UI (ui/src/features/rules/examples/examples.ts imports this) and the Rust regression test (crates/host/tests/rules_buildings_examples_test.rs runs each `body` through the REAL federation + rules.run path against the seeded buildings.db). Editing a body here that breaks the query fails CI, so the click-to-load examples can never silently drift from a path that actually runs. NEVER put `--` (or `---`) inside a `body`: the `//` uncomment strip turns `// ---` into a bare `---`, which Rhai reads as a reserved operator. Section headers with dashes belong in the TS/Rust source comments, not in these strings.

examples.title: Buildings · energy intensity (query only)
examples.summary: Rank buildings by kWh per m² from demo-buildings. The finding block is commented out — uncomment to raise one.
body: // Rank every building by energy intensity (total kWh ÷ floor area from the `area` site tag).
// SQL rides in a `backtick` raw string so it can span lines (a "double-quoted" string can't).
let rows = query("demo-buildings", `
SELECT s.name AS building,
ROUND(SUM(pr.value) / CAST(REPLACE(a.val,' m2','') AS DOUBLE), 2) AS kwh_per_m2
FROM point_reading pr
JOIN point p ON p.id = pr.point_id
JOIN meter m ON m.id = p.meter_id
JOIN site  s ON s.id = m.site_id
JOIN site_tag a ON a.site_id = s.id AND a.tag = 'area'
WHERE p.name = 'Energy kWh'
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, a.val
ORDER BY kwh_per_m2 DESC
`).records();
// Each row is a POSITIONAL array in SELECT order: r[0] = building, r[1] = kwh_per_m2.
// Raise a finding on anything over budget (UNCOMMENT the 5 lines to enable):
// for r in rows {
//     emit(#{ level: "warning", building: r[0], kwh_per_m2: r[1],
//             msg: r[0] + " is energy-heavy: " + r[1] + " kWh/m2" });
rows   // the ranked table is the result

examples.title: Buildings · energy intensity (stricter, query only)
examples.summary: Same query, a stricter 0.75 kWh/m² threshold. The finding block is still commented out — uncomment to raise one.
body: // Same intensity query — flag anything over a stricter 0.75 kWh/m2 budget.
let rows = query("demo-buildings", `
SELECT s.name AS building,
ROUND(SUM(pr.value) / CAST(REPLACE(a.val,' m2','') AS DOUBLE), 2) AS kwh_per_m2
FROM point_reading pr
JOIN point p ON p.id = pr.point_id
JOIN meter m ON m.id = p.meter_id
JOIN site  s ON s.id = m.site_id
JOIN site_tag a ON a.site_id = s.id AND a.tag = 'area'
WHERE p.name = 'Energy kWh'
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, a.val
ORDER BY kwh_per_m2 DESC
`).records();
// Each row is a POSITIONAL array in SELECT order: r[0] = building, r[1] = kwh_per_m2.
// Raise a finding on anything over the stricter budget (UNCOMMENT the 5 lines to enable):
// for r in rows {
//     emit(#{ level: "warning", building: r[0], kwh_per_m2: r[1],
//             msg: r[0] + " is over the 0.75 kWh/m2 budget (" + r[1] + ")" });
rows   // the ranked table is the result

examples.title: Buildings · energy intensity → alert (live)
examples.summary: Same query, but the finding block is LIVE: it raises an alert on every building over budget (lands in your Inbox).
body: // Same intensity query — this one RAISES an alert on every building over 1.0 kWh/m2.
// `alert` findings are pushed to your Inbox + Outbox; `emit` (above) stays in the result only.
let rows = query("demo-buildings", `
SELECT s.name AS building,
ROUND(SUM(pr.value) / CAST(REPLACE(a.val,' m2','') AS DOUBLE), 2) AS kwh_per_m2
FROM point_reading pr
JOIN point p ON p.id = pr.point_id
JOIN meter m ON m.id = p.meter_id
JOIN site  s ON s.id = m.site_id
JOIN site_tag a ON a.site_id = s.id AND a.tag = 'area'
WHERE p.name = 'Energy kWh'
GROUP BY s.id, s.name, a.val
ORDER BY kwh_per_m2 DESC
`).records();
// Each row is a POSITIONAL array in SELECT order: r[0] = building, r[1] = kwh_per_m2.
// Raise the finding (LIVE):
for r in rows {
alert(#{ level: "warning", building: r[0], kwh_per_m2: r[1],
msg: r[0] + " is energy-heavy: " + r[1] + " kWh/m2" });
rows   // the ranked table is still the result
